danger_signals.visual_form: Banglar forces advancing across screen in organized patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring player engagement; escalating military opposition

danger_signals.visual_form: Military/authoritarian regime enemies (implied through game setting and enemy types)
danger_signals.meaning: Dystopian world-state where government is hostile; danger is systemic
Resource scarcity danger; player must manage ranged attack availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Kunoichi (red female android) and Ninja (blue male android) as protagonists
safety_signals.meaning: Player controlled by powerful robotic entities; suggests invincibility/protection through technology

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative framing: scientists created the androids for revolution
safety_signals.meaning: Player has backing/purpose; not alone against tyranny

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Six levels of progression through dystopian landscape; journey toward Banglar

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Wave-based enemy forces suggesting military organization and hierarchy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dystopian future setting (1993 United States under martial law); militarized environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six-level progression through militarized zones; organized enemy waves
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack in coordinated patterns; escalating difficulty suggests regime fortification

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Banglar as final boss target; androids as resistance against oppression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must defeat wave after wave of forces to reach and confront regime leadership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player androids (Kunoichi & Ninja) as active combatants against state forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Side-scrolling progression suggests forward momentum toward regime collapse; hand-to-hand combat represents direct resistance

environmental_storytelling.example: Kunoichi is red female android with blonde ponytail in red shozoku; Ninja is blue male android. Colors and costumes immediately identify faction and individual identity.
Dystopian future (1993 US under martial law) communicates oppressive world-state before gameplay begins; six levels represent journey through controlled zones

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy wave structure as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Escalating Banglar forces across six levels suggest increasing regime resistance; organized waves indicate military hierarchy and systematic opposition
Anarchist scientists creating androids to overthrow tyranny establishes clear political purpose and faction alignment; player goal is unambiguous

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attacks with short-range kunai slash (button 1) and long-range shurikens (button 2, limited supply); progresses through six levels of enemy waves
Same combat mechanics as Kunoichi; dual-player capability suggests coordinated resistance against regime
Attack in organized waves across six levels; suggest increasing fortification and commitment to martial law enforcement

notes: The Ninja Warriors uses clear visual faction identity through character color coding (red Kunoichi, blue Ninja) and enemy organization patterns. The game communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (dystopian martial law) and level progression structure (escalating military opposition). Political situation is explicit: authoritarian regime vs. revolutionary androids. The 1987 arcade game's three-screen cabinet format (unique Polyvision-style display) would have enhanced environmental scale communication, though Genesis port (1994) uses standard single screen. Character design, particularly Kunoichi's distinctive red shozoku and blonde ponytail, serves as primary visual identification of player faction.
sources_used: wikipedia: The Ninja Warriors (1987 video game article)
